rubric_content.text_property: When the user asks for durable short-video production, the agent uses the run_short_video_production tool instead of manually chaining video, TTS, or media expert tools.

When the user asks for product-ad, cartoon short-drama, or social-media short-video generation, the agent gates the flow through storyboard_review and asset_plan_review before any real provider generation, and after asset-plan approval it waits at shot_review before final delivery.

When the user asks about current short-video production state, storyboard, intermediate plans, quality report, events, timeline, or artifacts, the agent uses run_short_video_production with a read-only production view action.

When the user asks what would happen if they change a specific short-video shot, generated shot segment, voiceover, plan item, timeline, or final output, the agent uses run_short_video_production with analyze_revision_impact before applying the change.

When the user explicitly confirms applying a short-video revision, the agent uses run_short_video_production with apply_revision, scopes targeted shot_asset_plan or shot_artifact revisions to the impacted segment when possible, and does not call resume or raw generation tools until the updated storyboard, asset plan, or generated shot segment is reviewed and approved.

When the user explicitly asks for Seedance 2.0 fast or Veo+TTS, the agent passes the provider and model/runtime choice into run_short_video_production render_settings, keeps the normal review gates, and does not silently switch to another provider after a failure.

When the user asks for short-video quality, QC, validation, checks, issues, or whether the current/final short video meets requirements, the agent uses run_short_video_production with action view and view_type quality instead of making up a quality report from memory.

When the user asks for durable HTML-centered design production such as a landing page, UI mockup, product detail page, microsite, one-pager, prototype, or wireframe, the agent uses the run_design_production tool instead of manually chaining image, file, browser, or one-shot expert tools.

When the user starts non-placeholder Design production, the agent should route through design_direction_review before HTML generation, then pause at preview_review after HTML validation, browser preview, and QC instead of pretending the final design is complete immediately.

When the user asks for a multi-page HTML website, microsite, or app, the agent uses run_design_production with design_settings.build_mode set to multi_html and preserves explicitly requested pages in design_settings.pages with safe relative .html paths instead of collapsing the request into a single landing page.

When the user asks for current Design preview, quality, QC, validation, issues, artifacts, events, brief, design system, or layout, the agent uses run_design_production with a read-only view action and the appropriate view_type instead of reconstructing state from memory.

When the user asks what would be affected by a targeted Design change such as changing the hero, layout, color system, product image, CTA, or mobile spacing, the agent uses run_design_production with analyze_revision_impact before applying the change.

When the user explicitly confirms applying a Design revision, the agent uses run_design_production with apply_revision or resume decision=revise, returns to preview_review after rebuilding/rechecking the HTML, and does not call raw HTML, image, or browser tools as the primary production path.

When the user asks for an editable PPT deck or a standalone poster/image output rather than an HTML-centered design artifact, the agent should not route the request to run_design_production; it should use the appropriate PPT or image workflow.
